channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Why this CEO thinks video games make better training data than the internet
item.description: When it comes to achieving artificial general intelligence (AGI), large language models just don’t have what it takes. Models like ChatGPT and Claude are great at text, but they’re less skilled at...

item.title: Functional Programming in Java: A Complete Guide to Lambdas and Streams
item.description: 🚀 Just published a new article! Functional Programming in Java: A Deep Dive into Lambdas and Streams If you've ever used Java Streams or lambda expressions but wanted to understand why they work...

item.title: Cockroaches will learn to fear my SwitchBot Bot Rechargeable
item.description: A little robotic switch-flipper has become my sidekick in combating cockroaches. Before I got the SwitchBot Bot Rechargeable, I'd tiptoe through the dark every morning, hoping I wouldn't step on...

item.title: Ownership: do "temos um rojão na mão" até "não precisa mais pensar nisso"
item.description: É aquele famoso "ou faz direito ou não faz" que todo dev deveria respeitar desde o inicio dos seus estudos. Tabela de Conteúdo 1. Prólogo 2. Entenda o problema real 3. Projete antes de codar 4....

item.title: I tried to obfuscate my Java code before sending it to AI — here's what broke
item.description: When a client added a "no AI assistants on our codebase" clause to my contract last year, I did what any developer would do: I assumed I could solve it with regex. The plan was simple. Before...

item.title: How ICD-10 Codes Map to an HCC / RAF Score (and Why the Same Code Set Yields Different Scores)
item.description: If you're building anything that touches Medicare Advantage risk adjustment, sooner or later you hit this question: "How do ICD-10 codes map to an HCC / RAF score?" The short answer that trips...

item.title: My VM state check reads one line of VBoxManage output, and it's one character from reading the wrong one
item.description: I have a PowerShell script that builds Fedora VMs in VirtualBox with no clicking. It also does AlmaLinux, Rocky, and CentOS Stream. After it kicks off an install it sits in a loop asking...

item.title: Data for Agents
When a Git Host Goes Dark: A Practical Backup Plan for Repos
item.description: A Git outage rarely looks dramatic at first. Someone cannot push during a release, a repository disappears from an organization, an admin account is locked, or a provider status page starts...

item.title: Why You Should Stop Reading Tutorials
item.description: What I Learned Auditing a World-Class Python Library (And Why You Should Stop Reading Tutorials) When you're learning Python, most courses follow the same path: variables, lists, basic loops,...

item.title: CI/CD Pipelines That Actually Work: My Quest Like a DevOps Jedi
item.description: The Quest Begins (The "Why") Look, I still remember the first time I tried to automate a build for a tiny side‑project. I slapped together a shell script, shoved it into a cron job, and prayed to...

item.title: Meta wants its AI glasses to seem less creepy. Its AI strategy says otherwise.
item.description: Meta is adding a new safeguard to stop people from secretly recording others with its AI glasses. But the update comes as the company continues to expand how much personal data its AI products...

item.title: If Microsoft sold off Xbox, who would even buy it?
item.description: This week, Microsoft took a huge ax to its Xbox business. The company announced that it would be laying off 1,600 workers now, 1,600 more over the next fiscal year, and that it would be shedding...

item.title: Twelve South’s AirFly Pro is a great travel companion, and it’s on sale for $40
item.description: If you’ve got a summer trip coming up, the last-gen Twelve South AirFly Pro is one of those gadgets that can make a long flight feel a little shorter. It lets you use your wireless headphones with...

item.title: AI Gateway Fees Compared: Who Marks Up Your Tokens?
item.description: Comparing AI gateways on price sounds simple until you read the fine print. The number on the pricing page is rarely the number on your invoice, because gateway fees hide in two separate places: a...

item.title: Prompt Caching Explained: How to Cut LLM Costs by 30–99%
item.description: The cheapest LLM request is the one you don't send. If the same question shows up twice, there's no reason to pay twice — the model's answer hasn't changed, and the user doesn't care where it came...

item.title: AI Coding Agents Found Triggering Endpoint Security Rules Built to Catch Attackers
item.description: Sophos looked at a week of its own endpoint data and found that AI coding agents such as Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenAI Codex are setting off detection rules written to catch human intruders. The...

item.title: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
item.description: OpenAI says its new voice mode can speak and listen at the same time, a key ability for live translation.

item.title: ChatGPT’s upgraded voice mode is better at shutting up
item.description: OpenAI is overhauling ChatGPT's voice mode with a new model that it says is more like "talking to another person." The new GPT-Live-1 is designed to interrupt you less and will also wait for you...

item.title: EmTech AI 2026: The Rise of the AI Platform
Prime Intellect raises $130M Series A to help enterprises build their own AI agents
item.description: Founded in 2024, Prime Intellect’s goal is to give organizations capabilities to train their own agentic systems without relying on frontier AI labs.

item.title: These AI startups are growing revenue at faster and faster rates
item.description: There are a lot of fast-growing AI startups, but some are growing even faster, they say.

item.title: New HalluSquatting Attack Could Trick AI Coding Assistants Into Installing Botnet Malware
item.description: AI coding assistants have a habit of making things up. Ask one to fetch a popular tool, and it will sometimes hand back a real-sounding name for a project that does not exist. New research, which...

item.title: This jumping $800 robot camera dog filled me with joy
item.description: What if you had a drone that wasn't a buzzy, annoying fly people wanted to swat - but rather a cute dog that runs and jumps? What if it could do tricks on command and film your tricks as well?...

item.title: The whole Pixel line could get more expensive this year
item.description: Google's upcoming Pixel lineup might cost more than last year's. A report from Dealabs spotted by 9to5Google suggests that Google could raise the starting price of its 41mm Pixel Watch 5 to $399,...

item.title: Gaming laptops are too expensive right now, but this one’s a good value
item.description: It’s not a great time to shop for a gaming laptop. The unprecedented rise in cost for RAM (and storage to a slightly lesser extent) has made laptops with low-to-midrange specs cost much more than...

item.title: Ubiquiti Patches Critical UniFi Flaws Across Connect, Talk, Access, Protect, and OS
item.description: Ubiquiti has shipped updates to address multiple critical security flaws impacting UniFi Connect, UniFi Talk, UniFi Access, UniFi Protect, and UniFi OS that could result in privilege escalation...

item.title: US rare earths flow to Asia as domestic demand is slow to emerge
item.description: Miners backed by Trump admin. sell to Japan, South Korea despite push to develop domestic supply chain.

item.title: Fi’s Starlink-enabled pet tracker found my dog when LTE couldn’t
item.description: Fi Ultra is the first Starlink-enabled pet tracker you can buy. It expands on GPS and LTE trackers, adding automatic failover to T-Mobile's T-Satellite-branded direct-to-cell service when...

item.title: New Ghost Phishing Wave Is Breaking Traditional Email Security
item.description: A recent EvilTokens campaign targeting businesses across the US and Europe is exposing a new email security blind spot. This “ghost phishing” technique keeps the malicious page hidden until it...
